task:
Build fresh implementation context for a workspace-scoped product catalog following the newly completed tenancy foundation. Do not edit. Read the current domain/application/infrastructure/API/frontend/test patterns. Recommend concrete model fields, ownership/tenant boundaries, SKU normalization/conflict semantics, soft archive behavior, exact files, and an implementation-ready meta-prompt. Keep prices, stock, categories, suppliers, editing, paging, and workspace switching out of scope.

## Acceptance Contract
Acceptance level: attested
Completion is not accepted from prose alone. End with a structured acceptance report.

- criterion-1: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

Finish with a fenced JSON block tagged `acceptance-report` in this shape:

residualRisks: SKU reuse after archive is intentionally recommended as forbidden; changing that later requires a deliberate filtered-index/restore-history design.
Current tenancy is one unambiguous Owner workspace and has no workspace switching or memberships beyond Owner.
Frontend has no configured test runner; feature verification will rely on typecheck/lint/build and manual flow unless test tooling is added later.
childReport.diffSummary: No implementation diff; wrote only the required context artifact.
reviewFindings: high: backend/src/InventoryFlow.Api/ExceptionHandling/GlobalExceptionHandler.cs:33-39 - SKU conflicts need an explicit 409 mapping and unique-index race translation to avoid 400/500 responses.

high: backend/src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/Tenancy/CurrentWorkspaceResolver.cs:16-22 - every Product read/write must be derived from this resolver and include WorkspaceId; client-selected tenant IDs or ID-only mutations breach isolation.
medium: frontend/src/app/router/index.tsx:48 - Products remains a protected placeholder and needs the planned page/API integration.

medium: backend/src/InventoryFlow.Infrastructure/Persistence/Migrations/20260718105902_AddWorkspaces.cs:54-70 - SQL Server-specific migration context requires SQL Server Testcontainers coverage for the Product migration/index.
